right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 916 evidence rows linking MAP2K1 (ENSG00000169032) to melanoma (MONDO_0005105), including clinical_precedence (score 1) for melanoma and metastatic melanoma, cancer_gene_census somatic_mutation evidence, and cancer_biomarkers affected-pathway evidence in cutaneous melanoma (lit. 25370473). GWAS Catalog lists 138 SNPs mapped to the MAP2K1 region (e.g. rs35414043 at 15q22.31), but all are intronic/intergenic to neighboring genes (LCTL, ZWILCH, RPL4) with MAP2K1 an upstream non-closest gene at ~55 kb, so germline GWAS support for MAP2K1 itself is weak; the target-disease link is driven by somatic mutation and clinical precedence.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (916 evidences, clinical_precedence+cancer_gene_census+cancer_biomarkers); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (138 SNPs, all intronic/intergenic)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker returns a single Human record for MAP2K1 as a marker: Natural killer T (NKT) cell in fetal kidney (normal cell, single-cell sequencing, supports=1). No melanoma/melanocyte or tumor-cell marker record was returned, so CellMarker provides no melanoma-tissue expression anchor for MAP2K1.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (total_records=1; NKT cell, fetal kidney, normal cell)

right_safety.finding: OpenFDA FAERS reports 6,718 total adverse-event reports for the MEK1 inhibitor trametinib (last_updated 2026-04-28), with serious reports present, indicating a substantial real-world safety signal for pharmacologic MAP2K1/MEK inhibition. CellMarker shows MAP2K1 flagged as a normal-cell (NKT) marker, consistent with broad physiological MEK expression and on-target toxicity risk in normal tissue.
right_safety.provenance: OpenFDA_search_drug_events (drug_name=trametinib; total=6718 reports, serious present); CellMarker normal-cell (NKT) record

right_patient.finding: search_clinical_trials returns 30 melanoma trials of trametinib, dominated by BRAF V600E/K-mutant cutaneous/metastatic melanoma (e.g. Phase III NCT01584648, COMBI-AD adjuvant NCT01682083 ~852 pts, NCT01597908 vs vemurafenib, NCT01245062 vs chemotherapy ~297 pts), plus BRAF wild-type combinations (NCT01941927). Clear biomarker-defined patient stratification (BRAF genotype, LDH) is established.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials (intervention=trametinib, condition=melanoma; total_count=30; multiple completed Phase III)

right_commercial.finding: Robust small-molecule commercial precedent: 30 melanoma trials including completed Phase III registration studies for trametinib (approved MEK1/2 inhibitor, often combined with dabrafenib). TheraSAbDab returns 0 antibody therapeutics against MAP2K1, expected for an intracellular kinase - the modality is small-molecule, not biologics.
right_commercial.provenance: search_clinical_trials (30 trials, Phase III registration precedent); TheraSAbDab_search_by_target (target=MAP2K1; count=0 antibodies)

tractability.finding: Pharos classifies MAP2K1 (UniProt Q02750, kinase family) as Tclin - the highest development level, meaning it is the target of an approved drug. Very low novelty score (0.0005278) and 399 publications confirm a mature, well-characterized, highly druggable target.
